title: sambucus intermedia f. neomexicana
intro: Sambucus intermedia f. neomexicana, commonly known as New Mexican elderberry, is a subspecies of the sambucus intermedia plant, belonging to the Adoxaceae family. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, offering several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of colds and flu, as well as reduce pain and inflammation. The therapeutic actions of New Mexican elderberry include acting as an immunomodulator, antispasmodic, and diuretic, making it a valuable remedy for various health conditions. Its bioactive constituents include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
benefits: Sambucus intermedia f. neomexicana has many benefits, such as reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant also possesses antimicrobial properties that help combat infections and promote wound healing. Additionally, its antiviral properties can help mitigate the severity of viral infections like the common cold and flu, while its antioxidant properties contribute to overall cardiovascular health and cancer prevention. By harnessing the therapeutic potential of this plant, individuals can experience improved quality of life, enhanced immune function, and a reduced risk of chronic diseases.
actions: Sambucus intermedia f. neomexicana has many therapeutic actions, such as anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which help alleviate symptoms of colds and flu, allowing individuals to recover faster and return to their daily activities. The plant's antimicrobial and antioxidant properties also promote wound healing and protect against cell damage, enabling people to maintain healthy skin and reduce the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles. Additionally, Sambucus intermedia f. neomexicana exhibits neuroprotective and antispasmodic actions, which can help alleviate symptoms of anxiety and depression, enabling individuals to manage stress and improve their mental well-being. By harnessing these therapeutic actions, individuals can lead healthier, more balanced lives and enjoy improved physical and mental health.
constituents: Sambucus intermedia f. neomexicana has many active constituents, such as alkaloids, flavonoids, glycosides, phenolic acids, and volatile oils, which provide a range of medicinal properties. These constituents include compounds like neomexicanol, sambunigrin, and sambunigrine, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, helping to alleviate symptoms of colds, sore throats, and skin irritations. The medicinal constituents of Sambucus intermedia f. neomexicana can improve people's daily lives by providing relief from pain and discomfort, reducing the severity of allergic reactions, and promoting overall well-being. Additionally, the flavonoids present in the plant, such as kaempferol and quercetin, have been found to have anti-cancer properties and may help protect against chronic diseases like heart disease and diabetes.
parts: Sambucus intermedia f. neomexicana has many medicinal parts, such as the flowers, leaves, and roots, which are used to treat various health conditions. The flowers, rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, are traditionally used to make a tea that helps alleviate fever and respiratory issues. The leaves, containing alkaloids and terpenoids, are used to treat skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The roots, rich in saponins and tannins, are used to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and stomach cramps, due to their astringent and anti-inflammatory properties.
preparations: Sambucus intermedia f. neomexicana has many herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to alleviate various health issues. Teas made from the leaves and flowers of this plant are used to treat fever, cough, and sore throat, as well as to aid in digestion and reduce inflammation. Tinctures, on the other hand, are used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to their expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. Infusions of the plant's flowers are also used as a natural remedy for menstrual cramps, anxiety, and insomnia.
side_effects: Sambucus intermedia f. neomexicana can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, nausea, and vomiting due to its high content of glycosides. Additionally, excessive consumption may cause allergic reactions, including hives, itching, and difficulty breathing in some individuals. In rare cases, prolonged use or high doses of sambucus intermedia f. neomexicana may lead to uterine stimulation, causing contractions and potentially inducing labor. It is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using this plant for medicinal purposes to avoid potential side effects.
